THE HEART OF THE SHOP

The Emissions Test Is Why Everything Else Runs

Texas eliminated the old safety inspection, but Harris County still requires an annual emissions test to renew registration — and it's the reason people find us in the first place. Two OBD machines run at the same time, so two vehicles are tested together, side by side. You stay with your car the whole time — no drop-off, no keys handed over — and most drivers are back on the road in under 10 minutes. Once you're here for the test, it's what opens the door to everything else the shop does: repair, oil change, and battery service.

02

Request an Appointment

No phone call needed. Fill this out and send it to us by email or text — great if you'd rather not call, including for Deaf or hard-of-hearing customers.

This opens your own email or messaging app with the details filled in — just hit send. We'll confirm your appointment back by phone, text, or email.

03

After Your Emissions Test

Passing the test is the hard part — turning that into your registration sticker is easy once you know your options. Here's every way Harris County drivers get it done.

FASTEST

Renew Online (Texas by Texas)

Once your passing result is on file, renew at Texas.gov using the Texas by Texas (TxT) service — available 90 days before your expiration and up to a year after. You get an emailed confirmation to keep in your vehicle while the physical sticker is mailed, usually within a couple of weeks.

NO WAIT

Grocery Store & AAA Partner Locations

If your registration isn't too far past due, many Kroger, H-E-B, and AAA locations can print your sticker on the spot. Bring your complete renewal notice, proof of insurance, and ID — your passing emissions result is checked electronically. Availability and rules vary by store and county, so it's worth calling ahead.

IN PERSON

County Tax Assessor-Collector Office

Walk into any Harris County Tax Office location (or an approved substation) with your renewal notice, insurance, and ID. If your emissions result hasn't shown up electronically yet, bring the Vehicle Inspection Report (VIR) we hand you after your test — it's valid proof for up to 13 months.

BY MAIL

Mail-In Renewal

Send your renewal notice, a copy of your VIR, a copy of your ID, and payment to the county tax office. Slower than the other options, but simple if you'd rather not go in person.

Keep your VIR (Vehicle Inspection Report) in the glovebox after your test — it's your proof of a passing result no matter how you renew, and you can also pull a copy anytime at mytxcar.org.
